Supermarket Simulator Guide

Product Tier List

Ranked by profitability, demand, and ease of management.

Tier S

  • Alcohol (Beer/Wine)Requires alcohol license. Highest profit per unit.
  • CigarettesRequires tobacco license. Consistent demand, high margin.
  • Electronics (Headphones, Chargers)High margin, slower turnover.
  • Frozen PizzaRequires freezer. Great margin + steady demand.
  • Energy DrinksHigh turnover + solid margin. Always stock.

Tier A

  • Soft Drinks (Soda)Volume driver. Restock constantly.
  • Chips & SnacksFast turnover, reliable profit.
  • Ice CreamRequires freezer. Good margin, seasonal spikes.
  • CoffeeSteady demand, decent markup.
  • Chocolate & CandyImpulse buys near checkout = extra sales.
  • CerealFamily staple, consistent mid-margin sales.

Tier B

  • BreadLow margin but essential. Customers expect it.
  • Milk & DairyPerishable, frequent restocks needed.
  • EggsPrice-sensitive. Keep markup low.
  • Canned GoodsLong shelf life, slow but steady.
  • Pasta & RiceBulk items, moderate margin.
  • Toilet PaperLow margin, high volume. Good filler.
  • Cleaning SuppliesModerate demand, decent markup.

Tier C

  • Fresh Produce (Fruits/Veg)High spoilage rate. Hard to profit early.
  • Baked GoodsVery perishable. Requires careful ordering.
  • Pet FoodNiche demand. Only stock if you have space.
  • MagazinesLow margin, very slow turnover.

Note: Tier rankings reflect general community consensus from Steam forums and Reddit. Your store's optimal mix depends on shelf space, licenses owned, and local customer demographics. Adjust based on your end-of-day sales reports.

Supermarket Simulator Guide — Unofficial fan site. Not affiliated with Nokta Games.
